nodes.name: OpenAI
nodes.summary: OpenAI is a company that develops and releases AI models, including GPT-5.

nodes.name: GPT-5
nodes.summary: GPT-5 is an AI model developed by OpenAI, requiring a mandatory enterprise license for API access.

nodes.name: Meta
nodes.summary: Meta is a company that releases open-source models, benchmarking within 8% of GPT-4 performance.

nodes.name: GPT-4
nodes.summary: GPT-4 is an AI model, used as a benchmark for Meta's open-source models.

nodes.name: Stanford
nodes.summary: Stanford is an institution where researchers have published findings on the impact of OpenAI's new pricing model.

nodes.name: Large corporations
nodes.summary: Large corporations are customers that will have advanced AI capabilities due to OpenAI's new pricing model.

nodes.name: Government agencies
nodes.summary: Government agencies are organizations that will have advanced AI capabilities due to OpenAI's new pricing model.

nodes.name: Small startups
nodes.summary: Small startups are companies that will be priced out of cutting-edge AI access due to OpenAI's new pricing model.

nodes.name: Academic researchers
nodes.summary: Academic researchers are individuals that will be priced out of cutting-edge AI access due to OpenAI's new pricing model.

nodes.name: Enterprise license
nodes.summary: Enterprise license is a mandatory license required for API access to GPT-5, priced at $200 per month.
